principle: "As everyone knows" is a logical trap: either obvious or a lie — replace with source
explanation: X.copy|false_certainty: "as_everyone_knows/no_secret/obviously" -> logical_trap; if_true -> why_write_it; if_false -> you_lied; NO:just_delete; replace_with_actual_source; when_contradicting_common_belief -> explain_origin_first eg: "obviously_neural_nets_won" >> "AI_job_postings_tripled_in_2024"

principle: Opinion markers imply previous dishonesty — delete unless contrasting two positions
explanation: X.copy|opinion_markers: "in_my_opinion/honestly/frankly" -> delete; "honestly"_implies_before_was_dishonest; keep ONLY when_contrasting: "survey_says_X + but_I_think_Y"; NO:habitual_hedging eg: "honestly_the_food_was_bad" >> "good_reviews_but_I_disliked_the_food"

principle: Oral clarifiers are speech crutches — write precisely the first time instead of self-correcting
explanation: X.copy|oral_clarifiers: "more_precisely/in_other_words/I_mean" -> oral_habit; reader_can_reread -> no_live_corrections_needed; NO:self-correcting_on_paper; write_right_first_time eg: "he_bought_a_smartwatch._Actually_grandma_gifted_it" >> "grandma_gifted_him_a_smartwatch"

principle: Verbal numbering is oral structuring — in writing use paragraphs or real numbered lists
explanation: X.copy|verbal_numbering: "firstly/secondly/finally" -> oral_structuring_tool; writing: one_paragraph == one_thought; numeric_list ONLY for instructions_or_referenced_items; NO:numbering_unordered_criteria eg: "1._truth 2._testability 3._rigor" >> "— truth — testability — rigor"

principle: Parentheses visually say skip me — extract important content or delete unimportant
explanation: X.copy|parentheses: content_in_brackets -> visually_demoted; important? -> extract_to_main_text; unimportant? -> delete; OK_only_for_citations_at_sentence_end; NO:hiding_key_info_in_brackets eg: "Vasily_(factory_director)_greeted_us" >> "factory_director_Vasily_greeted_us"

principle: "By the way" reveals the thought doesn't belong here — restructure or delete, don't patch
explanation: X.copy|by_the_way: "by_the_way/incidentally" == author_insecurity; relevant_thought -> place_in_natural_flow; irrelevant -> delete; NO:gluing_unrelated_ideas eg: "dont_eat_at_night._BTW_our_yogurt_1.5kcal" >> "our_yogurt_1.5kcal_—_safe_to_eat_at_night"

principle: Nested subordinate clauses signal author stress — split into sentences or compress to one word
explanation: X.copy|nested_clauses: clause_within_clause -> author_cramming_story_into_one_breath; often_stress/justification; many_commas == warning; fix: split_to_sentences OR compress_clause_to_adjective; NO:one_sentence_excuses eg: "child_who_is_sick_and_needs_treatment" >> "sick_child"
